company.name: Kymera Therapeutics
company.subtitle: Oral STAT6 degrader (KT-621) Phase 2b · IRAK4 w/ Sanofi · CDK2 w/ Gilead · IRF5 Phase 1

market.notes: First oral STAT6 degrader — replicates dupilumab ($13B+ peak) in pill form. Phase 1b AD: compelling early data at AAD 2026. BROADEN2 Phase 2b AD data mid-2027. BREADTH Phase 2b asthma data late-2027. If it works: oral dupilumab replacement across AD, asthma, EoE, CRSwNP, CSU. Potentially $5B+ peak across indications.

First-in-class oral IRF5 degrader. IRF5 is master regulator of B cell activation and autoantibody production. Preclinical: activity in lupus + RA models. Phase 1 started early 2026. Targets diseases where B cells drive pathology.
Partnered with Sanofi. KT-485 is 2nd-gen IRAK4 degrader (more potent than KT-474). Sanofi advancing. Kymera has US 50/50 profit split option + double-digit ROW royalties. Phase 1 expected 2026.

Exclusive option/license with Gilead. CDK2 molecular glue degrader for breast cancer. Gilead exercises option → option payment + Gilead takes global development/commercial. Kymera gets milestones + royalties.

mega_bear.narrative: STAT6 degrader doesn't replicate biologics — TPD platform overhyped
events: ✗ KT-621 Phase 2b AD misses — degradation insufficient vs dupilumab
✗ IRF5 and IRAK4 programs hit safety hurdles
✗ Gilead drops CDK2 option
→ TPD platform credibility damaged, stock collapses to cash ($22/share)

bear.narrative: KT-621 works but inferior to dupilumab — niche oral option only
events: ~ KT-621 Phase 2b shows signal but not dupilumab-equivalent
~ Oral convenience matters but efficacy gap limits adoption
✗ IRF5 slow to advance, IRAK4 delayed by Sanofi prioritization
→ Platform validated but commercial potential limited

base.narrative: KT-621 matches dupilumab orally + IRF5 enters clinic + Sanofi advances IRAK4
events: ✓ BROADEN2 AD data competitive with dupilumab — oral advantage
✓ BREADTH asthma confirms broad Type 2 platform
✓ IRF5 Phase 1 shows clean safety + target engagement
~ Sanofi advances IRAK4 into larger trials — milestones flowing

bull.narrative: Oral dupilumab replacement + multi-franchise degrader platform
events: ✓ KT-621 best-in-class oral Type 2 therapy — $5B+ peak across AD/asthma/EoE
✓ IRF5 validates in lupus — first-in-class mechanism
✓ Sanofi IRAK4 blockbuster — 50/50 US profit split to Kymera
✓ Gilead exercises CDK2 option — milestones + royalties

psychedelic_bull.narrative: TPD platform leader — oral biologics across immunology + oncology — Sanofi or Roche acquires
events: ✓ KT-621 replaces dupilumab globally — $8B+ oral Type 2 franchise
✓ IRF5 + IRAK4 create multi-target autoimmune franchise
✓ Platform generates 5+ clinical programs across undruggable targets
✓ Sanofi exercises full buyout or Roche acquires at $150+

lastData.title: KT-621 Phase 1b AD late-breaking oral at AAD
points: KT-621 Phase 1b AD data: late-breaking oral presentation AAD 2026
BROADEN2 Phase 2b AD ongoing — data mid-2027
BREADTH Phase 2b asthma first patient dosed Jan 2026 — data late-2027
KT-579 IRF5: Phase 1 dosing started early 2026
CDK2 molecular glue: Gilead option — preclinical ongoing
FDA Fast Track granted for KT-621 in AD

nextCatalyst.title: BROADEN2 Phase 2b AD data (KT-621 vs placebo)
thresholds: Primary: EASI-75 + vIGA response in moderate-severe AD
Cross-study comparison vs dupilumab Phase 2 data
Also late-2027: BREADTH asthma Phase 2b data
2026 interim: IRF5 Phase 1 safety/PK + IRAK4 Phase 1 (Sanofi)

risks: Primary data readout not until mid-2027 — 15+ months away
TPD (targeted protein degradation) is unproven modality at scale
Cross-study dupilumab comparisons are unreliable without head-to-head
KT-295 TYK2 program dropped — platform isn't infallible
Sanofi controls IRAK4 timeline — could deprioritize
Gilead CDK2 option may not be exercised
$3.7B market cap for a company with zero revenue and 2027+ readouts
Dupilumab patent cliff creates opportunity but also generics/biosimilars
